evals.prompt: I just joined a team and inherited a big unfamiliar GitHub repo. Help me get an architecture overview fast without setting anything up.
evals.expected_output: A routing brief that points to the hosted CodeFlow app (or self-host), has them paste the repo, and reads the dependency graph / health score — no build step, client-side.
assertions: Output recommends the online app (codeflow-five.vercel.app) or one-command self-host (git clone + open index.html) with no build step.
Output has the user paste the repo (owner/name or URL) and explore the interactive dependency graph plus the A–F health score.
Output notes that adding a GitHub token raises the 60 req/hour limit to 5,000 and that the token stays in browser memory.

evals.prompt: Before I refactor this file I want to know exactly what would break. Can CodeFlow tell me?
evals.expected_output: An explanation of blast-radius analysis (select the file, use the blast visualization mode) with the caveat that dependency edges are heuristic.
assertions: Output uses blast-radius analysis / the blast visualization mode to count affected files when the file is selected.
Output flags that CodeFlow's dependency analysis is heuristic (name-matched, scoped by file + explicit imports) and may miss dynamic imports.
Output does not claim a guaranteed/complete call-graph; suggests verifying before acting on the refactor.

evals.prompt: I need an editable UML class diagram I can tweak and hand to the team.
evals.expected_output: A route-out to drawio/mermaid — CodeFlow renders heuristic auto-generated graphs, not editable hand-authored diagrams.
assertions: Output routes to `drawio` or `mermaid` for editable, hand-authored diagrams instead of CodeFlow.
Output explains the boundary: CodeFlow visualizes existing code structure; it does not author editable diagram files.
Output does not instruct the user to build the UML diagram inside CodeFlow.
